summary_one_line: A default-namespace attribute (xmlns="..." with no prefix) parsed deep in SAX2, so xmlNewNs is called with prefix==NULL and takes the false branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML element carrying a default-namespace declaration `xmlns="<uri>"` (no prefix). The SAX attribute path must recognize the literal token x-m-l-n-s on an unprefixed attribute name (SAX2.c L1170/L1724 character gates name[0..5]=='xmlns') and pass a NULL prefix into xmlNewNs at SAX2.c:1214.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that get progressively closer on the byte-wise comparisons that gate the xmlns recognition path (the chained name[0]=='x' && name[1]=='m' ... equality cascade), so it incrementally assembles the literal `xmlns` attribute name and reaches the call site that passes prefix==NULL; the winner resolves the false branch with much higher hitcount (15 vs 1, and 89 vs 4).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive and cmplog lack the CMP_MAP distance signal; naive must hit the multi-byte `xmlns` literal by blind havoc, and cmplog's I2S substitution only fires when an operand value already appears in the input bytes, so neither gets steady credit for partial progress toward the unprefixed-xmlns token and they sit blocked (8/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets) gives partial-match coverage credit on the xmlns name-comparison cascade, letting it climb the gate that naive/cmplog cannot get steady feedback on.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid XML document with an element bearing a default namespace declaration `xmlns="..."`; the surrounding DOCTYPE/element scaffolding must be intact enough that the SAX attribute-internal path runs and calls xmlNewNs with NULL prefix.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire generalizes corpus entries into token/gap structures and splices them, so it recombines the `xmlns` attribute and the valid element/DOCTYPE scaffolding wholesale rather than byte-by-byte; the winning seeds were produced by GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator / GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator and reach the false branch with an extreme hitcount of 1959 vs cmplog's 1.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has I2S but no structural generalization; it can substitute logged operands at matching offsets but cannot assemble or preserve the larger valid-XML structure needed to repeatedly drive the namespaced-attribute SAX path, so it stays blocked (8/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural mutation (GeneralizationStage + Grimoire token/gap recombination) reassembles the namespaced-attribute structure that cmplog's I2S cannot build, vastly increasing reach of the prefix==NULL call.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same default-namespace `xmlns="..."` attribute reaching xmlNewNs(ctxt->node, val, NULL); the distinguishing feature is that xmlNewNs is reached via a particular SAX call context (xmlSAX2AttributeInternal at SAX2.c:1214) that context-sensitive coverage treats as new.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so reaching xmlNewNs through the default-namespace call path (xmlSAX2AttributeInternal at line 1214) is a new (call-context, edge) pair that gets preserved as corpus; this keeps inputs that drive the attribute path alive and it resolves the false branch (8/10, hitcount 79 vs 4).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive's plain edge-bucket feedback gives no extra credit for reaching xmlNewNs through the namespaced-attribute caller versus other callers, so the intermediate inputs that almost reach the call site are not retained and it stays blocked (8/10, hitcount 4).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook (call-context, edge) pairs) rewards reaching xmlNewNs through the default-namespace SAX caller as new coverage, retaining the path that bare naive discards.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is xmlNewNs's prefix-NULL test at tree.c:766, globally blocked on the false side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:     if (prefix != NULL)
Globally blocked side: F  (false branch)

evidence_trail.claim: The winning false branch is taken when xmlNewNs is called with a NULL prefix from the default-namespace SAX path.
The unprefixed xmlns attribute name is gated by a chained per-character equality cascade in the SAX attribute path.
value_profile wins over cmplog and over naive with the same value_profile delta and resolves at much higher hitcount than its losers.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=15  loser=1

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile vs naive is a decisive value_profile-delta pair with the winner resolving 9/10 and loser blocked 8/10.
value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient preserves inputs that get a CMP-operand pair closer to matching, which is the partial-progress credit on the xmlns cascade.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's I2S only substitutes an operand where the matching byte sequence already appears in the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire beats cmplog under the grimoire_structural delta and resolves the branch with an extreme hitcount of 1959 versus 1.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=1959  loser=1

evidence_trail.claim: The grimoire winning seeds were produced by Grimoire structural mutators.
grimoire's structural mutators splice and recurse on generalised token/gap structures (grammar-free structural mutation).
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx beats naive under the ctx_coverage delta, resolving 8/10 versus loser blocked 8/10.
naive_ctx folds the caller chain into the edge-map index so the same edge is a distinct slot per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: The default-namespace branch in xmlNewNs is reached only by the winner-resolving seed set in the caller (1214).

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Lineage verification is mandatory only for an I2SRandReplace claim; none of the four decisive winners (value_profile, grimoire, value_profile, naive_ctx) is I2S, so the primary claimed_mechanism for the highest-prob_div pair is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile). For corroboration I still ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 6925 --role W --fuzzer grimoire --trial 1 --seed e1d7963fe300eca8`: the chain shows GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator/GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator ancestors plus one dash row at depth 5; per the MECHANISM CONTEXT grimoire caveat that dash signal is NOT I2S-exclusive (grimoire wraps all stages nameless), so it does not change the per-pair structural/CMP_MAP attributions. value_profile emits no dash rows, consistent with a non-I2S win.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating xmlNewNs on a multi-byte literal `xmlns` comparison cascade where value_profile does NOT outperform naive (no partial-progress credit advantage) and grimoire does NOT outperform cmplog — i.e. all four winners tie their losers — would refute that these techniques each independently enable the prefix==NULL path.

weakest_evidence_point: The BYTE DIFF section reports no informative W-vs-L divergence, so the precise input bytes that flip the branch are inferred from source/caller structure (the xmlns name cascade) rather than observed directly in the seed diff.
